title: chenopodium ambrosioides f. spathulatum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides f. spathulatum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a plant species in the Chenopodium genus, characterized by its distinctive aroma and medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used for its antispasmodic, carminative, and diaphoretic properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues, nausea, and fever. The health benefits of Mexican tea include relief from menstrual cramps, indigestion, and bloating, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve overall digestion. Mexican tea can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and it is also used as an ingredient in traditional Mexican and Southwestern American cooking.
